Author: 曹培胜
Title: IDEA plugin 'github copilot' can't login in github
Text:
My IDEA plugin, GitHub copilot, can't login in GitHub. It is wating for GitHub Authentication...
But! My VSCode can login in successfully.
My IDEA plugin, GitHub copilot, can't login in GitHub. It is wating for GitHub Authentication...
But! My VSCode can login in successfully.
Also make sure u have successfully signed up with providing payment method :) (https://github.com/features/copilot) Intelij idea ignored my logins until I've configured it on GH side.
Update your github-copilot to the latest version (1.1.24.1610) then close IDEA.
Download this version of github-copilot (1.1.20.1417) and Extract it
Navigate to ...\github-copilot-intellij-1.1.20.1417\github-copilot-intellij\lib
Copy the core-1.1.20
file
Navigate to
…\AppData\Roaming\JetBrains\IdeaIC2022.1\plugins\github-copilot-intellij\lib
…\AppData\Roaming\Google\AndroidStudio2021.2\plugins\github-copilot-intellij\lib
Replace core-1.1.24
with core-1.1.20
.
It sometimes does this at least it happened today on PhpStorm 2023-1 EAP. Before it worked normally. I wonder if I would pay them the same way as it is working.
I had the same issue with my JetBrains products. Get to this website and activated Github copilet for my Account and it worked. https://github.com/github-copilot/free_signup
My issue is the same. Copilot works fine in VsCode but with IntelliJ, only the first log in when I install Github Copilot works. All following login actions failed.
My solution is to open C:\Users\<your computer name>\AppData\Local\github-copilot\hosts.json
(replace by your computer name) and you will see file hosts.json
Delete all data in this file and try to log in Github Copilot by IntelliJ.
You must delete this file data every time you reopen the IDE. It's manual but this is my best solution.
Hope it helps you
In my case caused by certificate issue.
How to know you have the same issue?
Fix:
I am writing to summarize various steps I took to resolve this problem. I started from this GitHub discussion on same issue. All below steps are general except few places which are specific to Windows OS.
First of all we need to check if there is any middleman / third party authentication which we can find using this SO answer. This is specially true in organization for security and compliance reason.
You can easily find your middleman
a. Go to Chrome browser, open any SSL website and click on SSL certificate section.
b. Now click on Connection is secure and select Certificate is valid.
c. In this dialog you will see the name of SSL issuer / middleman.
Now that we know issuer, we can get export the particular certificate from Certificate Manager as a .DER file
Once certificate is saved, we need to convert .DER
into .PEM
. We can do that by using certutil.exe
. Use this command to convert certificate.
certutil.exe -encode <der file> <pem file>
Now based on this post add the .PEM
file/certificate to IntelliJ IDE.
Make sure you have access to GitHub Copilot.
Install the GitHub Copilot plugin for JetBrains
After GitHub Copilot is installed, click Restart IDE.
After your JetBrains IDE has restarted, click the Tools menu. Click GitHub Copilot, then click Login to GitHub
In the "Sign in to GitHub" dialog box, to copy the device code and open the device activation window, click Copy and Open
A device activation window will open in your browser. Paste the device code, then click Continue.
GitHub will request the necessary permissions for GitHub Copilot. To approve these permissions, click Authorize GitHub Copilot Plugin.
After the permissions have been approved, your JetBrains IDE will show a confirmation. To begin using GitHub Copilot, click OK.
I'm using WSL2 and Webstorm for the first time, I noticed that it decided to update me to the latest 2025 build when I created a new WSL2 based project - I was also attempting to install CoPilot on the Host
- after downloading the latest nightly of the extension from https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/17718-github-copilot/versions/nightly and I couldn't get past the Waiting for Github Authentication
dialogue.
I then trying installing the latest extension from disk for the Client
and it worked first time.
